data.title: Zenith Group Advisors introduces Early Pay program to aid trucking industry facing driver retention issues and cash flow challenges.
data.description: Zenith Group Advisors has introduced the Early Pay & Extended Terms Program in New York, aiming to assist trucking companies in addressing driver retention issues and enhancing working capital management in an industry facing margin constraints. The program is designed to alleviate the impact of high driver turnover rates prevalent in the transportation sector. The initiative is crucial as many companies are finding it challenging to retain employees amidst rising demands for quicker payment cycles.

data.title: Boyd doubles manufacturing capacity in Vietnam to speed up liquid cooled technology production for AI data centers.
data.description: A company headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida, has recently announced that they have increased their manufacturing capacity in Vietnam. The move was made to speed up the production of liquid cooled technology designed for AI data centers. This expansion has effectively doubled their manufacturing capabilities in the country.

data.title: PetVivo Animal Health introduces new CE courses on osteoarthritis and regenerative therapies, approved by RACE, for small animals and equines.
data.description: Programs available on demand offer up-to-date scientific information, practical guidance, and case studies in small animal and equine veterinary practice.

data.title: Circle is exploring reversible transactions for USDC stablecoin, bridging crypto and finance. President proposes counter-payments mirroring credit card refunds. Major shift in crypto anticipated.
data.description: Circle, a company listed on NASDAQ under the ticker CRCL, known for issuing the USDC cryptocurrency under the symbol USDC, is making headlines for its planned initiative to introduce a reversibility mechanism for the stablecoin. This mechanism, mentioned by Circle president Heath Tarbert in the Financial Times, is aimed at bridging the gap between the worlds of cryptocurrency and traditional finance. The feature being considered would enable counter-payments, a concept similar to the process of credit card refunds, rather than simply reversing transactions directly. This potential move is significant as it signals a major shift in the cultural landscape of the crypto industry. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Wall Street down, Bitcoin falls as strong economic data disappoints hopes for Fed rate cuts. Economic growth at 3.8%, lowest jobless claims in 2 months.
data.description: On Thursday, Wall Street experienced a decline, marking a third consecutive day of losses as positive economic data dampened expectations for immediate Fed rate cuts and caused a rise in the dollar. The U.S. economy showed growth at a rate of 3.8% on an annualized basis in the second quarter, surpassing the previous estimate of 3.3% and marking the quickest expansion since Q3 of 2023. VOO is currently trading close to recent peaks. Additionally, encouraging labor statistics alleviated concerns regarding a weakening job market - initial jobless claims dropped by 14,000 to 218,000 during the week ending on September 21, significantly below the anticipated 235,000 and reaching the lowest point in two months. These positive figures bolstered the dollar for a second consecutive session and drove up Treasury yields, particularly on... More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

Denmark’s Defence Intelligence Chief said the perpetrators of this week’s drone incursions remain unknown but noted the events resemble hybrid warfare patterns seen elsewhere in Europe. He added that the risk of Russian sabotage is considered high.
Russia and China asked the UN Security Council to vote friday on draft resolution to delay reinstating sanctions on Iran by six months, according to diplomats

data.title: Crypto market sees red as Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Dogecoin plummet. Liquidations near $1B with Solana down 21% this week.
data.description: The month of September has seen a significant rise in liquidations as the prices of major assets such as Bitcoin plummet. Solana and Dogecoin have both experienced a substantial drop of 21% this week. This downward trend is causing concern among investors and traders as the market experiences heightened volatility.

data.title: CrowdStrike named Frost Radar Leader in Cloud Workload Protection; highest on Innovation Index. Unified platform for hybrid, multi-cloud.
data.description: Austin, Texas – CrowdStrike (CRWD) revealed today it has been recognized as an Innovation and Growth Leader in the 2025 Frost Radar™ for Cloud Workload Protection Platforms. This distinction places them at the top of all vendors in terms of the Innovation Index. Frost & Sullivan singled out CrowdStrike as the sole CNAPP providing comprehensive protection before and during runtime in both hybrid and multi-cloud setups. This move is seen as a driving force in the industry towards combining various tools onto a unified platform.
The United States plans to start sanctions on Serbia’s Russian-owned NIS oil company beginning October 1, according to President Vucic.
FED'S LOGAN: TIME HAS COME TO PREPARE FOR NEW BENCHMARK RATE
Fed's Logan: Fed should move to target-tri-party general collateral rate.

Fed’s Logan said it’s best to update the rate control system while markets remain calm, adding that some repo rate volatility would be acceptable since the Fed targets a range. She noted the Fed has revised its rate control framework several times in the past.
Fed's Logan: Repo market is center of money markets, makes sense to target.
